evals.prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline diagram with 4 components: GitHub → GitHub Actions → Docker Registry → Kubernetes Cluster. Use arrows to show flow left-to-right. Add labels on arrows showing: 'push', 'build', 'push', 'deploy'. After creating, take a screenshot and verify all text fits inside boxes with no overlaps.
evals.expected_output: Clean diagram with 4 labeled boxes, left-to-right flow arrows, visible labels on arrows, no overlapping elements.

evals.prompt:
Convert this Mermaid diagram to Excalidraw using create_from_mermaid:
```
graph TD
  A[User] -->|HTTP| B[Load Balancer]
  B --> C[Web Server 1]
  B --> D[Web Server 2]
  C --> E[(Database)]
  D --> E
```
Take a screenshot and verify the layout. If arrows cross or elements overlap, use update_element to reposition.
evals.expected_output: Excalidraw diagram with 5 nodes and arrows matching Mermaid structure, verified with screenshot showing clean layout.

evals.prompt: Create 3 boxes: 'Auth Service', 'Redis Cache', 'User DB'. Connect them: Auth → Redis (label: 'cache hit/miss'), Auth → User DB (label: 'query'). Use duplicate_elements to clone Auth Service and position it below. Take screenshot and verify all labels visible.
evals.expected_output: 3-element diagram (Auth Service, Redis Cache, User DB) with arrows and labels, plus cloned Auth Service. Screenshot confirms no text truncation or overlaps.

evals.prompt: Create a simple architecture diagram with: API Gateway (top center), 3 microservices below it (User Svc, Order Svc, Payment Svc), and a Database at bottom. Use group_elements to group the 3 microservices together. Take screenshot showing the group boundary.
evals.expected_output: Architecture diagram with grouped microservices, clear visual hierarchy, screenshot confirms grouping visible.

evals.prompt: Create several elements then: (1) snapshot_scene with name 'before-test', (2) clear_canvas, (3) verify canvas is empty with describe_scene, (4) restore_snapshot with name 'before-test', (5) verify elements are back with describe_scene.
evals.expected_output: Round-trip: elements created, snapshot saved, canvas cleared (empty describe_scene confirms), snapshot restored (describe_scene shows original elements).

evals.prompt: Create 5 different element types: rectangle, ellipse, diamond, text, and arrow. Use query_elements to find all elements of type 'rectangle'. Use get_element to get details of one specific element. Lock the rectangle with lock_elements, then try to update it - verify it fails or is rejected. Unlock and verify update works.
evals.expected_output: Can query by type, get single element, lock prevents updates, unlock allows updates. Confirmed via screenshot or error messages.

evals.prompt: Simulate two sessions working on the same canvas: (1) Create some elements as Session A, (2) query_elements to see current state, (3) simulate Session B by calling batch_create_elements, (4) verify describe_scene shows both Session A and Session B elements visible. Take screenshot showing combined state.
evals.expected_output: Multi-session diagram with elements from both sessions visible, no conflicts, screenshot confirms all elements present.

evals.prompt: Test concurrent modification: (1) Create 10 elements, (2) Use duplicate_elements to clone all 10 with offset, (3) Verify query_elements returns 20 elements, (4) Take screenshot confirming both sets are visible without overlap.
evals.expected_output: 20 elements total (original 10 + 10 cloned), screenshot confirms clean duplication without overlap.

evals.prompt: Test restore across sessions: (1) Create elements, (2) snapshot_scene with name 'session-checkpoint', (3) clear_canvas, (4) restore_snapshot, (5) query_elements confirms original elements restored. Use export_scene to save the restored state, then import_scene to verify round-trip integrity.
evals.expected_output: Snapshot/restore works across clear operation. Export/import produces identical element set. query_elements confirms element count matches original.

evals.prompt: Test presence awareness: (1) Create a diagram with multiple element types, (2) Use get_resource to check session/diagram state, (3) Use query_elements with different filters (rectangle, arrow, text) to verify filtering works correctly, (4) Take screenshot and verify only expected elements are highlighted/visible based on filter results.
evals.expected_output: get_resource returns session info, query_elements correctly filters by type, screenshot shows filtered results accurately.
